serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed octopus is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a medium-sized portion as a protein dish in a Singaporean meal.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Octopus — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Steamed Octopus (F03050028). All core macros and micronutrients reflect the HPB record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked octopus (NDB 15165) as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: N/A — Steamed octopus contains negligible digestible carbohydrates; GI is not applicable for this high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate seafood
gi_source.source_match_notes: Octopus and cephalopod molluscs are not listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. The carbohydrate content (4.4 g/100 g) is primarily shellfish glycogen, which has a negligible glycaemic effect in the context of a high-protein food matrix. GI is classified as not applicable per Veritas rules for negligible-carbohydrate seafood. Glycemic load per 150 g serving is effectively 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 460 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold; partially offset by exceptionally high potassium at 630 mg/100 g (Na:K ratio approximately 0.73, which is favourable)
hypertension.guidance: Limit portion to 100–150 g and avoid adding salt, soy sauce, or any seasoning; the high potassium content (630 mg/100 g) provides meaningful cardiovascular offset but does not fully neutralise the elevated sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high protein (29.82 g/100 g), negligible sugar (0 g/100 g), and low carbohydrate (4.4 g/100 g as shellfish glycogen) with no glycaemic spike risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed octopus is an excellent protein source for people with Type 2 Diabetes; consume without sweet sauces or marinades, and moderate portion if combined dyslipidaemia is present given cholesterol at 96 mg/100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol at 96 mg/100 g and sodium at 460 mg/100 g are the primary cardiovascular concerns; low saturated fat (0.46 g/100 g) and high potassium (630 mg/100 g) are protective factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portions to 100 g, avoid added salt or salty condiments, and note that the low saturated fat and high potassium profile are favourable despite the moderate cholesterol and elevated sodium content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed octopus (清蒸章鱼) is a minimally processed seafood dish prepared by steaming whole or sectioned octopus until the flesh is firm and dense, with a characteristic reddish-grey skin and white interior. The texture is chewy and dense, with a mild, slightly briny oceanic flavour. It is consumed across Singaporean, Chinese, Mediterranean, and East Asian cuisines as a high-protein protein dish. Nutritionally, steamed octopus is exceptional: it provides 29.82 g of protein per 100 g, making it one of the highest-protein seafood options available. Fat content is very low at 2.08 g/100 g with only 0.46 g saturated fat, making it suitable for low-fat dietary patterns. Carbohydrate content is 4.4 g/100 g, primarily as shellfish glycogen, with zero sugar, conferring no glycaemic spike risk. Iron content is notably high at 9.54 mg/100 g, supporting individuals at risk of iron-deficiency anaemia. Calcium at 106 mg/100 g and potassium at 630 mg/100 g provide additional mineral value. The primary clinical caution is sodium at 460 mg/100 g, which exceeds the 400 mg threshold relevant to hypertension management; however, the high potassium content provides a meaningful offsetting benefit. Cholesterol at 96 mg/100 g warrants moderation for individuals with combined dyslipidaemia. Steamed preparation avoids the formation of pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) associated with high-heat cooking methods. Steamed octopus is suitable for keto, high-protein, gluten-free, and dairy-free dietary patterns.
common_user_queries: How much protein is in steamed octopus?
Is steamed octopus good for diabetics?
Can I eat steamed octopus if I have high blood pressure?
What are the nutrients in steamed octopus?
Is steamed octopus suitable for heart disease?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F03050028) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown adapted from USDA FoodData Central cooked octopus (NDB 15165) as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions; this adaptation is documented. All other primary macros and micronutrients are exact HPB values.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.46 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (0.35 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated fat (0.59 g/100 g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked octopus (NDB 15165) as HPB Foodo does not separately report fatty acid fractions. Trans fat is confirmed 0.0 g by USDA source. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for F03050028 and detailed vitamin and mineral fractions for this specific steamed preparation are not available in the Singapore HPB database as of 2026. USDA FoodData Central (NDB 15165) provides partial vitamin data for generic cooked octopus but was not used to supplement these fields to avoid mixing source confidence levels without explicit flagging. 3. Glycemic index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable: steamed octopus is a high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate seafood food; the 4.4 g/100 g carbohydrate is primarily shellfish glycogen with negligible glycaemic impact. Octopus is not listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Setting glycemic_index=0 would be incorrect; null with not_applicable is the correct representation per Veritas rules. 4. Sugar is confirmed 0.0 g/100 g per HPB source — confirmed zero. Dietary fibre is confirmed 0.0 g/100 g per HPB source — confirmed zero. 5. Ontology: FoodOn URI and SNOMED CT code are defensible best-match entries; FoodEx2 code A00LQ is the closest defensible match for cooked octopus in the FoodEx2 hierarchy. Reviewers should verify FoodEx2 code against the EFSA FoodEx2 browser if exact code is required. 6. Aliases self-check performed: all 14 unique string values in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases array. No discrepancies found.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0 confirmed zero per HPB), sodium_mg (460.0), saturated_fat_g (0.46 adapted from USDA), sugar_g (0.0 confirmed zero per HPB) — all present as numeric values and plausible.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0) with gi_category 'not_applicable' per Veritas rule for negligible-carbohydrate seafood. gi_source documents University of Sydney GI Database consultation and confirms octopus is not listed. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. All four GI fields present and correctly handled. Previous iteration failure (glycemic_index was non-numeric) resolved by setting to JSON null.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided (FOODON_03411433, octopus food product, exact match). FoodEx2 code A00LQ (Octopus, cooked, exact match) provided. SNOMED CT 226888001 (Octopus food, exact match) provided. No codes invented; all are defensible. Reviewer note in notes field flags FoodEx2 for independent verification.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, texture, taste, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients including protein, iron, sodium, potassium, cholesterol, and carbohydrate profile.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate, sodium/potassium key factor, specific clinical guidance), Type 2 Diabetes (low, protein/carbohydrate key factor, specific guidance), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate, cholesterol/sodium/saturated fat key factors, specific guidance). human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly. Machine key type2_diabetes used.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Steamed Octopus (F03050028) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Two issues from iteration 2 have been resolved: (1) glycemic_index is now correctly set to JSON null with gi_category 'not_applicable' for this negligible-carbohydrate seafood, satisfying q4; (2) the flat aliases array has been rebuilt with exactly 14 unique entries that match verbatim all values in aliases_by_language, satisfying q8. Core macros are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions are adapted from USDA FoodData Central NDB 15165 and documented. All -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Multilingual naming satisfies the Singaporean requirement. Health context covers all three mandatory conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Ontology anchors are defensible. File is output as _pass.json.
